product_name: Tata AIA Param Raksha Life Maxima +
company: Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Limited
summary: A combination plan that provides life cover for protection, market-linked returns for wealth creation, and the Tata AIA Vitality Wellness Program. It is a combination of two individual products: Tata AIA Smart Sampoorna Raksha Supreme (UIN: 110L179V02) and Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance (UIN: 110N178V01).

quick_facts.sum_insured: Life Cover: Minimum ₹50 Lakhs, No Maximum Limit. Accidental Death Benefit (ADB) and Accidental Total & Permanent Disability (ATPD): Minimum ₹50 Lakhs, Maximum ₹5 Crore.
quick_facts.policy_term: 5 to up to 85 years of age for Limited Pay and Regular Pay

coverage: Life Insurance Cover: Lump sum benefit paid to family in case of unfortunate death.
Accidental Total & Permanent Disability: Benefit payout in case of Total and Permanent disability due to an accident. Includes 2X benefit for disability due to an accident in public transport.
Accidental Death: Additional Sum Assured in case of Accidental Death. Includes 2X Additional Sum Assured for accidental death in public transport.

Return on Maturity: On survival to the end of the policy term, the Total Fund Value valued at the applicable NAV on the date of Maturity will be paid, plus a return of Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance Premiums.
benefits: Participation in the Tata AIA Vitality science-based wellness program.
Upfront discount on Vitality Protect Advance premium in the first year.
Rewards for healthy lifestyle choices through the Vitality program.
Enhanced accidental benefits (2X payout) for incidents occurring in public transport.
Potential for Loyalty Additions, Refund of Mortality Charges, Twin Loyalty Booster, 2X Refund of Premium Allocation Charges, and Cover Continuance Booster.
eligibility: Minimum Entry Age: 18 years (age last birthday)
Maximum Entry Age: 65 years (age last birthday)
Maximum Age at Maturity: Life Cover up to 100 years; Accidental Death Benefit and Accidental Total & Permanent Disability cover up to 85 years.
Premium Payment Modes: Annual, Half yearly, Quarterly, and Monthly.
Premium Payment Term (PPT): Limited Pay and Regular Pay options, from 5 to up to 85 years of age.
Minimum Premium: ₹20,000 p.a. for Limited Pay 5-6 years, ₹18,000 p.a. for Limited Pay 7-9 years, and ₹15,000 p.a. for other Premium Payment Terms.
exclusions: Specific exclusions are not listed in the provided document. Please refer to the Policy Contract for precise terms and conditions.
claims_information: Visit the nearest Tata AIA Life branch office.

faqs.question: What is Tata AIA Param Raksha Life Maxima +?
faqs.answer: It's a combination plan that provides a life cover for protection, market-linked returns for wealth creation, and includes the Tata AIA Vitality Wellness Program.

faqs.question: Is this a single insurance product?
faqs.answer: No, it is a combination of two separate products: Tata AIA Smart Sampoorna Raksha Supreme (a Unit Linked Insurance Plan) and Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance (a Health Insurance Plan).

faqs.question: Who bears the investment risk in this plan?
faqs.answer: In this policy, the investment risk in the investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder.

faqs.question: Can I withdraw money from this plan in the first few years?
faqs.answer: No, linked insurance products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. You will not be able to surrender or withdraw the money invested, completely or partially, until the end of the fifth year.

faqs.question: What are the main benefits offered by this plan?
faqs.answer: The main benefits are a lump sum life insurance cover, accidental death and disability benefits, market-linked returns at maturity, and a wellness program with rewards.

faqs.question: What is the minimum and maximum entry age?
faqs.answer: The minimum entry age is 18 years and the maximum entry age is 65 years (age last birthday).

faqs.question: What is the maximum age of coverage under this plan?
faqs.answer: The life cover extends up to 100 years of age. The Accidental Death Benefit (ADB) and Accidental Total & Permanent Disability (ATPD) cover is available up to 85 years of age.

faqs.question: What happens in case of the policyholder's death?
faqs.answer: In case of the unfortunate death of the policyholder, a lump sum life insurance benefit is paid to the family or nominee.

faqs.question: What are the benefits related to accidents?
faqs.answer: The plan provides a benefit payout for Accidental Total & Permanent Disability and an additional Sum Assured in case of Accidental Death.

faqs.question: Are there special benefits for accidents in public transport?
faqs.answer: Yes, the plan provides a 2X benefit in case of disability due to an accident in public transport and a 2X Additional Sum Assured for accidental death in public transport.

faqs.question: What do I get when the policy matures?
faqs.answer: On survival to the end of the policy term, you will receive the total fund value at the applicable NAV on the date of maturity, plus a return of the Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance premiums paid.

faqs.question: What is the Tata AIA Vitality Wellness Program?
faqs.answer: It is a science-based wellness program that helps you improve your health and rewards you for making healthy lifestyle choices.

faqs.question: Are there any discounts with the Vitality program?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can get an upfront discount on the Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance premium in the first year, for instance, up to 10%.

faqs.question: What are the available premium payment modes?
faqs.answer: Premiums can be paid Annually, Half-yearly, Quarterly, or Monthly.

faqs.question: What is the minimum annual premium?
faqs.answer: The minimum premium depends on the premium payment term. For a Limited Pay term of 5-6 years, it is ₹20,000 p.a.; for 7-9 years, it is ₹18,000 p.a.; and for other terms, it is ₹15,000 p.a.

faqs.question: What are the premium payment term (PPT) options?
faqs.answer: The plan offers Limited Pay and Regular Pay options, with terms ranging from 5 years up to 85 years of age.

faqs.question: Are the returns from this plan guaranteed?
faqs.answer: No, some benefits are variable and non-guaranteed. Returns are based on the future performance of the opted funds, and the investment risk is borne by the policyholder.

faqs.question: How are potential returns shown in the brochure?
faqs.answer: The illustrations show two different assumed rates of future investment returns, 8% and 4%, as set by the Life Insurance Council. These assumed rates are not guaranteed.

faqs.question: What is the minimum and maximum Sum Assured for Life Cover?
faqs.answer: The minimum Sum Assured for Life Cover is ₹50 Lakhs, and there is no maximum limit specified.

faqs.question: What is the Sum Assured range for Accidental Death and Disability benefits?
faqs.answer: The minimum Sum Assured is ₹50 Lakhs and the maximum is ₹5 Crore for both Accidental Death Benefit (ADB) and Accidental Total & Permanent Disability (ATPD).

faqs.question: Are taxes applicable on premiums and benefits?
faqs.answer: Yes, all premiums, charges, and interest are exclusive of applicable taxes, cesses, or levies, which are borne by the policyholder. Any taxes will also be deducted from the benefits payable.

faqs.question: Can I buy the underlying products separately?
faqs.answer: Yes, the two component products, Tata AIA Smart Sampoorna Raksha Supreme and Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance, are also available for sale individually.

faqs.question: What are the UINs for the component products?
faqs.answer: The UIN for Tata AIA Smart Sampoorna Raksha Supreme is 110L179V02, and for Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance is 110N178V01.

faqs.question: How can I contact Tata AIA for claims or complaints?
faqs.answer: You can contact your Insurance Advisor, visit a Tata AIA Life branch, call 1-860-266-9966, or email customercare@tataaia.com.

faqs.question: What does the maturity benefit consist of?
faqs.answer: The maturity benefit includes the Total Fund Value, plus return of Tata AIA Vitality Protect Advance Premiums. It is also stated to be inclusive of Loyalty Additions, Refund of Mortality Charges, and other boosters, if any.

faqs.question: What is the risk of terminating the policy early?
faqs.answer: The document states that an early termination of the policy usually involves high costs and the surrender value payable may be less than all the premiums paid.

faqs.question: Who manages the investment funds for this ULIP component?
faqs.answer: The funds are managed by Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Ltd.
